items.reason: All 20 decision areas listed in `docs/decisions/decisions-needed.md` (DEC-001 through DEC-020) are realized in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` with id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences, and a non-empty projects_to. The decision-trace JSON block at the bottom of the file ties every decision to dictionary fields, and `check_dictionary_artifacts.py` enforces those traces.

`docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md` lists PEND-001 and PEND-005, each with description, why-it-matters, blocker, owner, and target_date (2026-05-15 and 2026-05-03 respectively). Research date is 2026-04-24, so neither target is past. Cross-references `DEC-012-runtime-coverage-per-spec` and `DEC-015` for the implicit choices.

Every sampled decision's consequences section starts at least one bullet with 'Removes the need for...' / 'Eliminates...' / 'Removes...' and names the concrete artifact deleted (e.g. DEC-001 'Removes per-function subject-to-person merge branches from `supabase/functions/lti-launch-handler/index.ts`...', DEC-007 'Removes duplicate source-ID columns from every public table'). Coexisting surfaces (e.g. enrollment vs membership in DEC-004) include explicit 'Why not collapse' justification.

Sampled decisions present three credible options including a more-aggressive simplification, e.g. DEC-001 considers 'Collapse every user, actor, profile, and credential subject into one person' (a real LMS shape), DEC-005 considers 'LTI AGS line items and results as the only canonical gradebook model', DEC-016 considers 'Commit only the shared dictionary and generate all spec views on demand'. None of the bookends are paper tigers.

`scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` reads `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` and emits the five `dictionary/*.v1.json` projections; the same script's `--check` mode is wired into VERIFY.md and into `check_dictionary_artifacts.py`. Seed reports 59 projected objects and 574 fields with per-spec totals matching the committed projections.

`canonical.identity.person.email` in `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` lists projections to `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.email`, `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_actor.email`, `dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership.email`, and `dictionary/qti-core.v1.json#qti_candidate.email` — the four-way email overlap the rubric calls out. Other canonical objects (course, class, role.family, outcome.line_item) follow the same pattern.

`canonical_objects[]` in `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` defines `canonical.identity.person` with its own fields (id, display_name, email, source_subject_id, identity_kind), allowed values, decision_id `DEC-001-person-agent-subject`, and explicit projections to OneRoster Person, Caliper Actor, QTI Candidate, and LTI launch/membership records. Unification is data, not just prose.

Each per-spec dictionary contains a `shared_enums[]` section keyed by `shared_enum_id` with both `crosswalk` (native→canonical) and `reverse_crosswalk` (canonical→[native_values]) entries (e.g. `grading_progress` maps OneRoster `fullyGraded` and LTI `FullyGraded` to canonical `fully_graded`). `check_dictionary_artifacts.py:check_shared_enum` enforces this bidirectional structure.

`scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` defines `INVALID_PRIVACY_CLASSES = {'depends_on_contents', 'depends_on_entity'}` and `validate_privacy_class` rejects any field/object using a placeholder or anything outside the closed seed list (DEC-019 fixes the keys to public/operational/directory/education_record/behavioral/sensitive/credential/privacy_governance/system).

`scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py` reads OneRoster runtime objects (`relationships[]` with relationship_key, source_field, target_object, cardinality, ownership, delete_behavior) from the seed and emits `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql`. `--check` is in VERIFY.md and `check_dictionary_artifacts.py` calls it for drift, so the SQL is generated, not hand-edited.

Every spec field carries `decision_id`; `check_dictionary_artifacts.py:check_dictionary_shape` enforces this and verifies the decision_id appears in the decision-trace JSON inside `standards-overlap-decisions.md`. Shared enums, canonical objects, and unsupported_or_deferred entries all carry decision_id references.

Five generator scripts (`generate_oneroster_core.py`, `generate_qti_core.py`, `generate_case_core.py`, `generate_caliper_core.py`, `generate_integration_governance_core.py`) emit Markdown + HTML dictionary pages and OpenAPI from the per-spec JSON. `check_dictionary_artifacts.py:check_artifacts` cross-checks every field's description, decision_id, and sourceStandard against the generated SQL, OpenAPI, Markdown, and HTML; running the generators on a clean checkout would produce zero diff or VERIFY.md fails.

The decision register renders as HTML at `site/docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html` with anchors per decision, and `docs/lead-spec-accounting.md` includes a 'Decision Citation Map' tying each Lead-spec accounting entry to its decision. However, several decisions (e.g. DEC-013 audit response truth, DEC-014 static mirror policy, DEC-016–DEC-020 dictionary/migration direction) are referenced only by ID in generated tables; there is no plain-language doc page that walks a reader through 'why a separate agent from person' or 'why CASE is canonical' beyond the decision register itself, which is the exact failure mode the substance_check calls out.
items.traced_cause: docs/architecture-why.md (or per-decision pages under docs/decisions/): write plain-language explanations for each DEC-XXX naming the choice and the work it deletes, rather than relying on the decision-register markdown as the only narrative.

items.reason: `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` covers all five required steps and links every field/enum to dictionary anchors (e.g. `oneroster-core-dictionary.html#person.email`, `enum.role_family.teacher`). However, every step uses raw `psql "$SUPABASE_DB_URL"` heredocs instead of the platform's API surface, so the guide is not 'against the live runtime' the way the rubric means — there are no curl examples against PostgREST/Edge Functions, step 4 instructs the reader to `create table public.build_app_assignment_case_links`, and step 5 hand-rolls a Caliper-shaped JSON from `audit_log` rather than reading a real Caliper feed.
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: replace direct `psql` heredocs with curl commands against `https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co/rest/v1/...` and the documented Edge Functions; for step 4, expose CASE alignment as a dictionary-backed table (or document a real CASE association endpoint) instead of asking the layperson to invent `build_app_assignment_case_links`; for step 5, expose a Caliper feed read endpoint rather than reconstructing events from `audit_log`.

items.reason: `site/api/site-link-check.json` reports `status: pass`, 0 errors, 199 local links checked, 633 dictionary entries verified, 20 decisions verified, 12 API endpoints checked, and confirms the buildability guide is reachable in 1 click. All 36 HTML pages are reachable within the 3-click budget from `site/index.html`.

A layperson reading only `docs/` cannot ship the five-step scenario: (1) every step requires `$SUPABASE_DB_URL`, but `docs/supabase-hosted-database.md` explicitly says 'the database connection string and API keys stay local in `.env`' and `.env.example` ships `SUPABASE_DB_URL=` empty — there is no documented way for a non-admin to obtain it; (2) the guide writes directly to `public.organizations`, `public.people`, etc. with a hard-coded tenant UUID `33333333-...`, bypassing the OAuth/RLS story DEC-010/DEC-015 describe; (3) step 4 instructs the reader to `create table public.build_app_assignment_case_links` because the platform has no CASE alignment surface; (4) step 5 fakes a Caliper feed by inserting an `audit_log` row and joining it back, rather than calling the Caliper ingestion/read endpoint. Each is a real gap, not a styling issue.
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md plus docs/supabase-hosted-database.md: re-author the guide against the public REST + Edge Function surfaces with a documented credentialing path (publishable key + a way for the layperson to obtain a tenant-scoped JWT), expose dictionary-backed CASE alignment and Caliper feed endpoints, and remove the assumption of admin DB access.

items.reason: `scripts/evaluate_platform.py` emits `traced_cause` on every non-pass item via `ensure_traced_causes` + `normalize_traced_cause`, and includes `buildability_gaps` with a per-gap `traced_cause` via `normalize_buildability_gaps`. The prompt and post-processing both require the field, with `DEFAULT_TRACED_CAUSES` as a backstop so empty traces are impossible.

VERIFY.md ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, which runs every iteration. The script writes counts, per-item traced_cause, buildability_gaps, projections summary, and a `done` flag to `site/api/platform-evaluation.json`.

`scripts/codex_loop.py:read_overrides` reads `docs/loop-overrides.md` at the top of each iteration; `overrides_paused` checks for a bare `PAUSE` token outside HTML comments and halts before launching the next iteration; `format_overrides_for_prompt` strips the comment template and injects the rest into the Codex prompt under a 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE...)' section.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` checks `after_eval.get('done')` after each iteration and returns 0 when true; it also returns 0 if the evaluator already reports `done` before any work. Hard caps (`--max-iterations`, `--stall-limit`) act only as safety rails, not as gates.

buildability_gaps.gap: Layperson cannot obtain $SUPABASE_DB_URL. The build guide assumes it is exported, but docs/supabase-hosted-database.md explicitly keeps the connection string and API keys local, and .env.example ships SUPABASE_DB_URL= empty. There is no documented self-service way for a new developer to get a tenant-scoped credential.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/supabase-hosted-database.md plus docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: document a public credentialing path (publishable key + a way to mint a tenant-scoped JWT) and switch the guide to use that instead of an admin Postgres URL.

buildability_gaps.gap: Step 1 inserts directly into public.organizations / public.people with a hard-coded tenant UUID, bypassing OAuth scopes, audited reads, and RLS. A layperson app developer would not have, and should not need, direct DB write access to ship roster data.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: rewrite step 1 as curl POSTs against PostgREST or a dictionary-backed Edge Function; surface organization/person creation as a documented API governed by the OAuth scope policies in dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json.

buildability_gaps.gap: Step 2 again uses raw SQL inserts and depends on the same admin-only DB URL, with no API alternative documented.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md and supabase/functions/: expose class/enrollment write endpoints (e.g. an Edge Function or PostgREST insert path scoped by JWT) and document them in the buildability guide.

buildability_gaps.gap: Step 3 inserts into public.line_items and public.results via psql. The platform already has a gradebook-bulk-submit Edge Function but the buildability guide does not call it.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: replace the SQL block with a curl call to https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.functions.supabase.co/gradebook-bulk-submit using a publishable key + user JWT, matching the example already in docs/supabase-hosted-database.md.

buildability_gaps.gap: Step 4 instructs the reader to `create table public.build_app_assignment_case_links` because the platform exposes no CASE alignment surface. That is an app-owned workaround, not a platform feature, and contradicts DEC-006 (CASE is canonical).
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: data/data-dictionary.seed.json + dictionary/case-core.v1.json + docs/build-an-edtech-app.md: model an `assignment_case_alignment` (or equivalent) object in the canonical dictionary with FK to line_item.id and case_item.uri, generate the migration via DEC-020, and document the resulting endpoint.

buildability_gaps.gap: Step 5 fabricates a Caliper feed by inserting one row into audit_log and joining it back into a JSON shape, rather than reading a real Caliper event store. The platform admits Caliper raw-event storage is deferred (DEC-012), so there is no read endpoint to use.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md PEND-001 (Caliper raw-event projection): bring the deferred Caliper read slice forward, then update docs/build-an-edtech-app.md step 5 to call the real feed endpoint instead of synthesizing one from audit_log.

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. QTI and CASE are doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated Edge Function receipt/governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, Caliper raw-event projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
